data.narrative:
Evozyne is a Series B biotech company founded by Andrew Ferguson, Rama Ranganathan, and Jeff Aronin, having raised $144M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 8 private-stage biotech companies and 3 public peers in the Biotech & Health sector.

Two methods triangulate to a defensible valuation range. Stage-progression analysis, applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement to the $144M raised proxy, implies $274M. Peer-median analysis across five same-sector, same-stage comparables—Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M)—yields a median of $810M, with a range of $600M–$3.3B. Public-comp anchoring is not applicable; while 3 public biotech tickers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) show a median P/S multiple of 7.8x, Evozyne's revenue is not on file.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $233M (low) to $931M (high), with a median estimate of $542M. The wide dispersion reflects meaningful uncertainty around Evozyne's competitive positioning relative to the peer set, which spans nearly a 5x spread. Stage-progression and peer-median methods weight toward different anchors; resolution will depend on deal terms, investor demand, and pipeline validation milestones.
